ip_outcome_note: No suspected_ip flag on this card; vision pass did not flag the depicted armored figures as a named lore character. Per spec anti-confab rule, generic armored figures in an M21 art do not get retro-fitted to a named NPC absent direct evidence (oracle/flavor text naming, Wizards story spotlight tying the art, or Scryfall card-page notes). None of those exist for this card, so the depicted figures remain anonymous.

trivia_bullets.text: Card details: {4}{W} sorcery, Core Set 2021 #35, common (the vision-pass MD lists rarity 'C'); oracle text 'Exile target nonland permanent. Its controller creates a 1/1 white Soldier creature token.' Illustrated by Zoltan Boros. [Scryfall (m21/35) via MTGAZone card listing; MTGNerdGirl M21 White Limited Review]

Flavor text (verbatim): "You know it's a great show when the Azorius arrest the props, too!" — Judith, the Scourge Diva. The speaker is the Cult of Rakdos's performance-artist grand dame from Ravnica Allegiance, an in-universe theatrical voice commenting cross-faction on the Azorius (white-blue law guild) — which fits the card's white-blue-glow exile imagery despite the card itself being mono-white. [Scryfall card page; MTG Wiki: Judith]

Artist Zoltan Boros is one of the most prolific MTG illustrators with ~248 cards in the Gatherer database; he worked for 25 years in the 'Boros-Szikszai' duo with Gabor Szikszai before going solo as a freelancer in 2010, contributing to MTG, D&D, Hearthstone, WoW, and Diablo. [MTG Wiki: Zoltan Boros; Gatherer artist index]

Mechanical reputation: rated a 3/5 in MTGNerdGirl's M21 white limited review as a 'typical 5-mana white exile' whose upside is hitting any nonland permanent (including planeswalkers), with the downside being the 1/1 token gift if used on a creature. EDHREC reports ~0.01% inclusion across 530 decks (effectively unplayed in Commander); reviewers note more efficient white removal exists. Community sentiment per the limited reviews: 'fine, most white decks like to play a single copy', not a build-around. [MTGNerdGirl review; EDHREC]

Set context: Core Set 2021 (M21) is the 2020 summer Core Set anchored thematically by Teferi (the set's face planeswalker and the cover-art figure for several premium products). M21 is a 'core' product positioned as an evergreen on-ramp set rather than a top-down plane-specific narrative, which is why a flavor quote from a Ravnica character (Judith) is paired with art that isn't tied to any specific named NPC — core sets routinely re-use cross-block flavor voices on generic-illustration cards. [Scryfall set page; MTG Wiki Core Set 2021]
